title: Macramé market bag
subtitle: A knotted net shopping bag in cotton macramé cord with doubled handles
excerpt: Knot a reusable market bag in 3 mm natural cotton macramé cord using alternating square knots to create a net structure that expands to hold bulky items. The finished bag has a flat base, doubled handles, and holds about 4 kg comfortably.
sourceNotes: Synthesised from traditional macramé knotting practice. Net shopping bags in knotted cord are documented in European craft traditions; the alternating square knot net is a standard beginner macramé structure.

glossaryTerms.definition: A net structure made by knotting groups of four cords into square knots on one row, then regrouping cords from adjacent knots to make the next row. Creates an open mesh.

A flat symmetrical knot made with two working cords around two filler cords. The left working cord crosses over the filler cords and under the right working cord; the right cord then crosses under the fillers and through the left loop.
A wrapping knot made by a single cord coiling multiple times around a group of cords. Used here at the base of the bag to cinch the net cords together.

content.text: A macramé market bag is built from a ring of
that forms the net body, cinched at the base with a
are the only knot type used in the main body; if you can make a square knot consistently, you can make this bag.

items.name: Metal or wooden ring, 5 cm diameter (for the top loop)

content.text: Cut 24 cords, each 4 metres long. Fold each cord in half and attach to the ring using a lark's head knot. You will have 48 working cord ends hanging from the ring. These are the cords for the bag body.

Work in the round. Divide the 48 cords into groups of 4 and tie one square knot in each group, about 3 cm below the ring. On the next round, regroup: take 2 cords from one knot and 2 cords from the adjacent knot and tie a square knot 3 cm below the previous row. This is the alternating square knot net structure. Continue for 10 to 12 rounds of alternating knots; the bag body will be about 30 cm deep.

Gather all 48 cord ends together at the base. Using a separate length of cord, wrap a gathering knot around all the cords 3 cm from the last knotted row. Wind the wrapping cord 8 to 10 times tightly, then secure the ends inside the wrap. Trim the cord ends below the gathering knot to a fringe of 5 to 8 cm, or tie each pair in an overhand knot close to the gather and trim.

Cut 4 cords each 1.2 metres long. Group them in pairs and braid or twist each pair into a handle. Attach each handle to the ring at opposite sides, threading the ends through the ring and securing with overhand knots inside the ring.

items.problem: The bag base does not close properly and has a large hole
items.cause: The alternating square knot rows were too widely spaced, leaving a gap too large for the gathering knot to close neatly
items.fix: Reduce the row spacing to 2 cm rather than 3 cm for the last three rows as you approach the base. A tighter mesh at the base prevents a large hole from forming when the cords are gathered.

items.problem: The square knots are all different sizes
items.cause: Inconsistent tension: some knots were pulled tightly and others loosely
items.fix: After each square knot, hold both working cords and pull them firmly and evenly before moving on. Practice on a sample piece to find the correct pull tension, then maintain it throughout.
